programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "A Taste of Life Part 1"
programme.desc: Why does Sardinia stand alone as Europe's Healthiest Community? Is it the coast, the pastures or the mountains that grant its residences with longevity?

programme.title: Health Explored S2 - "A Taste of Life Part 2"
programme.desc: Learn about the foods and the culture that makes Sardinia so special. From fishing with their hands, to traditional foods... it's a culinary exploration in good health.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "The Himalayas: Surviving the Summits"
programme.desc: The Himalayas run across 1550 miles of north India. The Himalayas' rugged highlands offer little direct refuge to humans, and in the shadow below, over a billion people in India rely on the mountains for survival.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "Western Ghats: Monsoon Mountains'"
programme.desc: The Western Ghats are a spine of mountains that lay claim to being one of the most biodiverse places in the world. In the east, tigers and wild dogs compete for prey. All the while, the western Ghats hold the key to life across southern India.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "India’s Lost World"
programme.desc: Northeast India is an anthropological paradise; there is no other place on Earth with so many different ethnic groups. The forests are filled with mysterious tribes whose lives are dictated by the rain and the seasonal fruits of the forest.

programme.title: Amazing Animals S1 - "Animal Journeys Part 1"
programme.desc: Extraordinary land, sea and air migrations. Voyages in the natural world that will leave you awestruck. What compels animals and even certain plants to embark upon such great journeys, pulling off remarkable navigational feats along the way?

programme.title: Amazing Animals S1 - "Animal Journeys Part 2"
programme.desc: Extraordinary land, sea and air migrations. Voyages in the natural world that will leave you awestruck. What compels animals and even certain plants to embark upon such great journeys, pulling off remarkable navigational feats along the way?

programme.title: Python Hunters S1 - "The Perfect Storm"
programme.desc: The Python Hunters decide to tackle the debate over how the Burmese Pythons came to be established in the Everglades.

programme.title: Python Hunters S1 - "Dying to Survive"
programme.desc: A cold snap has hit the state of Florida and frigid temperatures are killing thousands of native and non-indigenous animals of all species. Has Mother Nature solved their python problem?

programme.title: Python Hunters S1 - "Everglades Under Siege"
programme.desc: The Florida Everglades are home to thousands of invasive plant, insect and animal species and yet the Burmese Python is perceived as the biggest concern. Are they a problem or is this snake hysteria?

programme.title: Python Hunters S2 - "Snake Invasion"
programme.desc: This time on Python Hunters… It’s a new season of hunting, and the guys are back in the Everglades to see if Florida’s bitter winter has put a serious dent in the Burmese python population.

programme.title: Wildlife Heroes S1 - "Birute Galdikas"
programme.desc: Dr. Biruté Mary Galdikas is the leading expert on orangutans, having devoted her life to saving them over the last 50 years.

programme.title: Wildlife Heroes S1 - "Christina Karliczek"
programme.desc: Christina Karliczek Skoglund has been filming animals on land and under water in the most remote places for over ten years.

programme.title: Wildlife Heroes S1 - "Dr. Betsy Fox"
programme.desc: Originally from the USA, Dr. Betsy Fox has lived in Namibia since 1990 and has led an exciting life ever since amongst its wildlife.

programme.title: Bright Now S1 - "Raising a Genius"
programme.desc: Every child has a gift, and all of us as humen beings have a contribution that we can make to society, and we just have to find what that is. But what does it take to create a genius? What should the education of a genius look like?

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Surviving a Bear Attack"
programme.desc: Nick and Laura create kick-ass tests to explore the immense power and startling speed of grizzly bears. They use Hollywood stunt tools to experience an attack. Then try to recreate a “miracle” arrow shot that saved a man from being killed by a bear.

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Surviving Treestand Accidents"
programme.desc: In this exciting episode we’ll find out the reasons why tree stand accidents happen and how we can prevent them. Cool science mixed with dynamic experiments send our hosts, our stunt team and our special crash test dummies plummeting to the ground.

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Sniper Attack"
programme.desc: Military snipers are trained in a variety of skills: detection, stalking, camouflage, infiltration, special reconnaissance and target acquisition. Bottom line, a ton of cool shooting and science for Nick and Laura to explore!

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Surviving a Lightning Strike"
programme.desc: Laura and Nick travel to a state-of-the-art Direct Effects Lightning Test Lab. With some “explosive” experiments, they’ll determine the power lightning and how it behaves? And they’ll find out how can hunters and fishermen best protect themselves?

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "Terry Reacher"
programme.desc: We're putting Jack Reacher to the test! The movie, the series, and the pie!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "The Harder Terry Falls"
programme.desc: Dust off your chaps as Terry tests several things from the epic western The Harder They Fall, and a canteen buster test from Gunsmoke!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "The Schappert Identity"
programme.desc: Terry channels his inner Jason Bourne to test some of the best moments from the series of films.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S6 - "We Do It For You"
programme.desc: We go to the mail room for viewer requests as Terry and Larry take a look into famous scenes from Yellowstone, SWAT, Fast and Furious.

programme.title: The Re-Inventors S2 - "Panjandrum"
programme.desc: Rockets, 100 pounds os explosives, and a "wheel of fiery death". For the Re-Inventors it's just another day, another danger.

programme.title: The Re-Inventors S2 - "Windmill"
programme.desc: Over 1000 years old windmill design. Is it still usable today?

programme.title: The Re-Inventors S2 - "Crematorium"
programme.desc: Crematorium heated by sunlight, find out if the sun can turn a person into a pile of ashes.

programme.title: The Re-Inventors S2 - "Flame Thower"
programme.desc: A thousand years old Chinese flamethrower, will it be a real weapon or just a kitchen tool for flaming.

programme.title: The New Frontier S2 - "Shore to Shore"
programme.desc: Our seafaring nature has translated well to the space age, seeking to sail out into the unknown in search of riches of one kind or another. While Europe and Asia continue their interest in the resource rich Moon, The United States and NASA on Mars.

programme.title: The New Frontier S2 - "Mars: Adapt or Leave"
programme.desc: As the latest successes and failures come and go Mars continues to give up her secrets. Invisible clouds are revealed with ultraviolet light and the search for the source of methane continues with ExoMars.

programme.title: The New Frontier S2 - "Emissaries"
programme.desc: Our solar system is vast, from our own star the Sun to Earth is nearly 150 million kilometers or 1 Astronomical Unit. Jupiter is 5.2 AU distant and Pluto up to 48AU, the solar system extends far beyond this into interstellar space.

programme.title: The New Frontier S2 - "Danger Asteroids Ahead"
programme.desc: The question often rises is the cost of space exploration really worth it? Could the money be better spent elsewhere. If so one space program would have to remain. The planetary defence program, to locate identify and deflect a wayward asteroid.

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Surviving Nature’s Fury – Storms"
programme.desc: Nick and Laura experience Nature’s fury through storm amazing generating gear that simulates terrible hurricanes, ice cold winds and even tornados. For these experiments they’ll use remarkable technology that creates massive storms on Hollywood sets.

programme.title: Survival Science S1 - "Surviving a Mountain Lion Attack"
programme.desc: Laura and Nick explore the techniques to deal with these apex predators when they are the most dangerous. We’ll scientifically breakdown the behaviors, strength, eyesight and speed of mountain lions through outrageous experiments, in day and night.

programme.title: Nigel Latta Blows Stuff Up S1 - "Fire"
programme.desc: What really happens in a house fire ? Nigel sets out to get the answer by setting a house on fire. The results are surprising.

programme.title: Nigel Latta Blows Stuff Up S1 - "Space"
programme.desc: Nigel wants to be the first New Zealander to make it into space. To do so he explores the fundamental physics of rocket science, and demonstrates the sometimes wayward nature of science.

programme.title: Nigel Latta Blows Stuff Up S1 - "Sound"
programme.desc: Breaking the sound barrier. A good excuse for Nigel to invent some crazy experiments, and break a few other things along the way. Using some clever models, including 150 school students with flags, he examines the fundamentals of sound.

programme.title: Nigel Latta Blows Stuff Up S1 - "Gravity"
programme.desc: Gravity is usually associated with names like Galileo and Isaac Newton. Nigel tests the fundamentals of gravity through some interesting and innovative experiments.

programme.title: Science of Thrills S1 - "Speed"
programme.desc: Engineer and adventurer Rob Bell climbs aboard some of the world’s fastest theme park rides and learns what it takes to build them.

programme.title: Science of Thrills S1 - "Falling"
programme.desc: Engineer and adventurer Rob Bell discovers how our fear of falling is exploited by some of the world’s top theme park rides.
